steps.title: Spot the compound description
steps.content: The blank should contain a two-part description of Gerald Clarke—his titles “artist” and “2007 Eiteljorg Fellowship recipient”—joined by the conjunction “and”.

steps.title: Apply the compound noun punctuation rule
steps.content:
When you join two nouns with a single “and” to create a compound description, never use a comma before the “and”—that eliminates choice A.

Additionally, don't insert a comma between the description and the person's name when they appear in sequence; a comma would only be correct if it appeared on both sides of the name (setting it off as a full appositive), but there's no opening comma here.

Choice C's hyphen is nonstandard.

Choice B correctly joins the two titles without extraneous punctuation and keeps them directly attached to the name.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
